reasoning: The Wikipedia page 'FIDE rankings' tracks the official FIDE rating lists, which are published exactly once a month (on the 1st of the month). The table for top female players explicitly states it is based on the 1 March 2026 rankings. Therefore, the information on the Wikipedia page is expected to remain static throughout March, and the ranking for Nana Dzagnidze on March 22 will be identical to her ranking on March 15 (which is 19). Because 19 is 'as high or higher' (equal to) 19, the resolution will be YES. The only reasons it would be NO would be page vandalism exactly on the resolution date or a highly non-standard retrospective edit to the March 1 list, making the YES outcome practically certain.
